title: Is the university an enabling environment? A qualitative study examining the impact of university disability policy for staff
abstract: This study aims to address the university social responsibility (USR) in France by examining disability policy for staff. The concept of an enabling environment is utilized, grounded in the capabilities approach. The aim is to understand how a French university fosters an enabling environment, on the one hand, to facilitate the disclosure of disability and, on the other hand, to promote participation in the institution’s disability policy. The present study employs a qualitative methodology, encompassing both primary and secondary data collection. A thematic content analysis is then conducted, utilising QDA Miner software. The results demonstrate the processes ranging from mobilizable resources to achievements. Some positive conversion factors are highlighted (e.g., the visibility of disability ambassadors), but also some negative ones (e.g., lack of time). It is evident that capabilities are observed, thereby strengthening the power of staff to take steps towards recognition as a disabled worker or to engage in the university’s disability policy (e.g., participation in DuoDay).

analysis.中文摘要: 研究以法国某大学员工残障政策为对象，采用“赋能环境”和能力方法，探讨大学如何促进员工披露残障身份并参与残障政策。研究使用一手和二手资料，进行定性主题内容分析。结果显示，从可调动资源到实际成就存在一系列过程，积极转换因素包括残障大使的可见性，消极因素包括时间不足；这些因素影响员工获得残障工作者认定或参与相关政策活动。
analysis.研究主题: 大学残障政策、赋能环境、员工参与、能力方法
analysis.空间/场景类型: 大学组织与制度环境；物理空间未明确说明
analysis.研究场景: 法国大学中的员工残障政策情境
analysis.自变量: 残障政策、可调动资源、转换因素、残障大使可见性、时间不足
analysis.因变量: 残障身份披露、残障工作者认定、参与大学残障政策
analysis.行为指标: 采取残障认定步骤、参与DuoDay、参与残障政策
analysis.生理/感知指标: 能力感或赋权相关表现；具体生理指标未明确说明
analysis.研究方法: 定性研究、 primary和secondary数据收集、主题内容分析、QDA Miner软件分析
analysis.数据/样本: 法国某大学相关一手和二手资料；具体样本量未明确说明
analysis.主要结论: 大学残障政策可通过资源和积极转换因素增强员工能力，促进残障身份认定和政策参与；但时间不足等负面因素会限制参与。
analysis.与建筑/体育空间/疗愈环境研究相关性: 边缘相关。论文关注大学中的制度性和社会性赋能环境，而非建筑空间、体育空间、疗愈环境或VR空间体验；但其关于残障员工参与、包容性政策和赋能环境的定性分析可为校园无障碍与包容性环境研究提供间接参考。
analysis.可借鉴启发: 可借鉴“赋能环境”和能力方法，用于分析大学或公共机构中环境资源、转换因素与残障群体参与行为之间的关系；也可参考其定性主题分析方法评估包容性政策对使用者行动能力的影响。

title: Bridging the Gap in Reading Skills: The Role of Home Literacy Environment and Parental Expectations in Chinese Rural-to- Urban Migrant Children
abstract: Background Research on the Home Literacy Environment (HLE) in China has predominantly centreed on disparities between urban residents (UR) and rural-to-urban migrants (RUM), yielding substantial insights while leaving critical gaps unaddressed. Although existing literature has identified socioeconomic status (SES) as a key determinant of HLE disparities, it has frequently overlooked pivotal factors such as the duration of urban residency and parental expectations (PE) regarding children’s reading proficiency. These variables are integral to developing a holistic understanding of HLE dynamics within RUM families. Furthermore, while the adverse effects of such disparities on low-level reading skills (LLRS) among migrant children are well-established, potential variations in high-level reading skills (HLRS) remain underexamined. Reading constitutes a multifaceted cognitive process involving textual meaning construction. Grounded in theoretical frameworks such as the he triangle model and the dual-route model, LLRS, encompassing vocabulary, morphological awareness, and phonological awareness, serve as foundational competencies for basic text decoding. In contrast, HLRS, including comprehension monitoring, comprehension control, and reading comprehension, integrate and build upon these foundational skills. This research gap assumes heightened significance during the transition from home-based to school-based literacy environments in early elementary education, a pivotal developmental phase characterised by substantial contextual shifts in literacy acquisition. Last but not least, while the influence of HLE on LLRS is widely recognised, its nuanced impact on HLRS, particularly throughout these transitional periods, remains insufficiently delineated. Equally underexplored is the role of PE in shaping these developmental trajectories. This oversight underscores the necessity for a more comprehensive inquiry into how migration status, in conjunction with HLE and PE, differentially influences the development of both LLRS and HLRS. Purpose Grounded in Bronfenbrenner’s Ecological Systems Theory and Family Investment Theory, this thesis seeks to systematically examine disparities in HLE between RUM and UR families, with a particular focus on how migration status, PE, and HLE collectively contribute to differential development in both HLRS and LLRS. The research comprises three interrelated empirical studies: Study 1 conducts a comparative analysis of HLE disparities between RUM and UR families, while assessing the extent to which duration of urban residency, SES, and PE shape the HLE of RUM households. Study 2 investigates discrepancies in HLRS between RUM and UR children, with a specific emphasis on the mediating roles of HLE and PE in explaining these differences. Study 3 expands the scope by incorporating both LLRS and HLRS, analyzing how migrant status indirectly influences HLRS through the mediating mechanisms of LLRS, HLE, and PE. By adopting a multi-study framework, this thesis aims to provide a nuanced understanding of the interplay between structural factors (e.g., migration status, SES), family-level influences (HLE, PE), and children’s reading development across differentiate skill levels. Methods The first study adopted a mixed-methods research design, utilizing a randomly selected sample comprising 148 UR and 137 RUM children along with their respective parents. The second study expanded the investigation with a larger participant pool of 311 children (163 RUM and 148 UR), employing independent samples t-tests and structural equation modeling to analyse group differences in HLRS while examining the potential mediating effects of PE and HLE. Building upon the same cohort, the third study similarly applied independent samples t-tests and SEM, but extended the analytical framework to incorporate both LLRS and HLRS, with particular emphasis on evaluating the mediating roles of LLRS, PE, and HLE in the association between migrant status and HLRS development. Results Study 1 revealed that, after controlling for SES, RUM children demonstrated significantly less favorable HLE compared to their UR counterparts, irrespective of duration of urban residence. However, longitudinal urban residency was positively associated with enhanced parent-directed literacy activities among RUM families. PE emerged as a robust predictor of literacy engagement, serving as a partial compensatory mechanism for the detrimental effects of low SES. Study 2 findings indicated that migrant status exerted a significant negative influence on both PE and HLE. Notably, when accounting for the combined effects of PE and HLE, the direct association between migrant status and children's HLRS was rendered statistically non-significant. This pattern suggests that migrant status primarily affects HLRS indirectly through its impact on PE, which subsequently influences HLE. A noteworthy dual pathway emerged: while the direct effect of PE on HLRS was negative, the mediated pathway through HLE demonstrated a positive association. This finding highlights that elevated PE, when not accompanied by commensurate HLE enrichment, may paradoxically hinder HLRS development. Study 3 results established that UR children consistently outperformed RUM children across multiple literacy domains, including reading comprehension, comprehension monitoring, phonological awareness, and morphological awareness. The analysis revealed an adaptive process wherein migrant parents gradually modify their educational expectations in response to evolving socioeconomic circumstances, subsequently adjusting their family's literacy resources and engagement patterns. These adaptations significantly influence children's LLRS, which serve as critical precursors for HLRS development. More precisely, the results delineate a sequential mediation pathway: migrant status affects HLE through PE, which in turn impacts LLRS, ultimately influencing HLRS outcomes. Conclusion and Implication This thesis presents several key findings. Firstly, even after accounting for SES, RUM families display significantly lower levels of parent-directed activities compared to UR families，due to their limited local social networks. However, with increased duration of residence, RUM families may gradually access better educational resources, showing improvements in literacy activities. PE, rather than SES, emerged as a significant predictor of home literacy activities, indicating that high PE can partially offset the negative effects of low SES on the HLE. Secondly, RUM children significantly trail behind their UR peers in both LLRS and HLRS. Initial analyses suggested a direct effect of migrant status on HLRS; however, this effect diminishes when considering indirect pathways through PE, and HLE. Thirdly, compared to direct socioeconomic factors, intermediary mechanisms such as the HLE and PE have a greater impact on children's reading abilities. LLRS, PE, and the HLE play a crucial mediating role between migrant status and HLRS. Migrant status indirectly affects LLRS through PE's impact on HLE, which subsequently influences HLRS. Notably, while the initial impact of PE on children’s reading abilities might be negative, it can turn positive through the influence of HLE. This sequence demonstrates how various factors collectively shape children's reading outcomes. This thesis provides valuable theoretical and practical insights into the factors influencing the reading development of migrant children. By identifying critical mediating factors such as PE and HLE, it enhances understanding of how to effectively support the literacy development of migrant children. Firstly, educational policies should focus on allocating resources that enrich literacy environments within migrant communities, such as access to libraries and digital resources. Secondly, there is a need for parental education programmes that inform migrant parents about the impact of their expectations and guide them in supporting their children's literacy in feasible ways. Additionally, establishing community support structures that offer educational support and resources can bridge the gap between home and school literacy practices. Lastly, developing school curricula that acknowledge and incorporate migrant children's cultural and linguistic backgrounds can improve their engagement and comprehension. These findings have significant implications for educational interventions and policies aimed at reducing the literacy gap between migrant and urban-resident children, both within China and potentially in other similar global contexts.

analysis.中文摘要: 论文考察中国城乡流动儿童与城市居民儿童在家庭读写环境、父母期望及阅读技能发展上的差异，并分析迁移身份、社会经济地位、城市居住时长、家庭读写环境和父母期望如何通过中介路径影响低阶与高阶阅读技能。
analysis.研究主题: 家庭读写环境、父母期望、迁移身份与儿童阅读技能发展
analysis.空间/场景类型: 家庭读写环境；家庭到学校读写环境转变；物理空间特征未明确说明
analysis.研究场景: 中国城乡流动儿童与城市居民儿童的家庭和早期小学读写发展情境
analysis.自变量: 迁移身份、社会经济地位、城市居住时长、父母期望、家庭读写环境
analysis.因变量: 家庭读写环境、低阶阅读技能、高阶阅读技能
analysis.行为指标: 家长主导读写活动、读写参与、家庭读写资源与参与模式、儿童阅读表现
analysis.生理/感知指标: 父母对儿童阅读能力的期望；生理指标未明确说明
analysis.研究方法: 混合方法研究、独立样本t检验、结构方程模型、中介效应分析、多研究框架
analysis.数据/样本: 研究1包括148名城市居民儿童和137名城乡流动儿童及其父母；研究2和研究3包括311名儿童，其中163名城乡流动儿童、148名城市居民儿童
analysis.主要结论: 城乡流动儿童家庭读写环境显著不如城市居民儿童，城市居住时长与家长主导读写活动提升有关。父母期望能预测读写参与，并部分补偿低社会经济地位影响。迁移身份主要通过父母期望、家庭读写环境和低阶阅读技能间接影响高阶阅读技能。

analysis.可借鉴启发: 可借鉴其将环境因素、家庭行为、期望变量和发展结果纳入结构方程模型的思路，用于分析空间环境如何通过行为参与或心理预期影响结果；但可迁移性限于方法层面，物理空间变量未明确说明。

title: Assessing the Impact of Extreme Climate Events on Cardiovascular Disease in 157 Chinese Cities (2015-2020): A Spatial and Causal Analysis.
abstract:
Introduction

Extreme climate events worsen cardiovascular disease burden, exacerbated by rapid aging. Prior studies have linked extreme climate events to cardiovascular disease mortality but have lacked city-level spatial extreme climate event impact data and causal evidence for extreme climate event-related cardiovascular diseases in individuals. This study explored the spatiotemporal patterns of 3 extreme climate event types (heat, cold, and precipitation) in 157 Chinese cities (2015-2020) and verified their causal effects on individual incident cardiovascular disease risk.

Methods

A repeated cross-sectional (spatial analysis) and longitudinal cohort (causal inference) mixed design was used. Extreme climate events were defined using 2 thresholds: absolute (heat ≥38°C, cold ≤ -10°C, and extreme precipitation >50 mm) and relative. Subsequent analyses and results were based on the absolute threshold, which provided a better model fit. Spatial autocorrelation was used to identify cardiovascular disease clusters, multiscale geographically and temporally weighted regression was used to quantify extreme climate event city-level cardiovascular disease associations, and dynamic double machine learning was used to examine individual causality/heterogeneity.

Results

Northeast China's cities were found to be sites of increased cardiovascular disease incidence. Heat extreme climate events increased city-level cardiovascular disease prevalence (east-west weakening impact). Each additional heat extreme climate event day was correlated with 1,128 more cardiovascular disease cases per 100,000 people. Cold extreme climate events increased city-level cardiovascular disease prevalence (west-east weakening impact). Each additional cold extreme climate event day was correlated with 391 more cardiovascular disease cases per 100,000 people. Causal inference analysis showed that each heat extreme climate event day increased cardiovascular disease risk by 3.044%; cold extreme climate event days increased risk by 0.110%, and precipitation extreme climate event days increased it by 1.620%. Heterogeneity highlighted high-risk subgroups in which heat extreme climate events affected preretirees, smokers, and those in high-ozone areas, whereas higher BMI values mitigated risk. Cold extreme climate events impacted preretirees, high-BMI individuals, and high-ozone populations. Precipitation extreme climate events most affected older adults, rural residents, preretirees, and spouseless individuals.

Conclusions

Heat and cold extreme climate events increased city-level cardiovascular disease prevalence in middle-aged and older adults with distinct spatiotemporal gradients. Exposure to heat, cold, and precipitation extreme climate events significantly increased incident cardiovascular disease risk, with clear subgroup vulnerabilities.

analysis.中文摘要: 该研究分析2015-2020年中国157个城市热、冷、极端降水事件的时空分布，并评估其对城市层面心血管疾病患病率和个体发病风险的影响。结果显示热、冷和降水极端气候事件均会增加心血管疾病风险，且存在空间梯度和脆弱人群差异。
analysis.研究主题: 极端气候事件对心血管疾病的空间分布与因果影响

analysis.研究场景: 中国157个城市，2015-2020年，城市层面空间分析与个体纵向队列因果分析
analysis.自变量: 热事件、冷事件、极端降水事件天数；绝对阈值为热≥38°C、冷≤-10°C、极端降水>50 mm；臭氧水平、BMI、年龄、城乡、婚姻状态等用于异质性分析
analysis.因变量: 城市层面心血管疾病患病率；个体新发心血管疾病风险
analysis.行为指标: 吸烟状态作为高风险亚组变量；空间使用、移动轨迹、活动行为未明确说明

title: Temporal variations of the gut microbiome in human health.
abstract: The colonisation of the human gut microbiome commences at birth and continues to evolve throughout the lifespan. A balanced symbiotic relationship between the host and gut microbiome is essential for maintaining overall health. This two-part Series presents a comprehensive overview of the gut microbiome across temporal and spatial dimensions, considering diurnal, seasonal, and lifespan variations while covering the entire gastrointestinal tract. We also discuss the extrinsic and intrinsic factors that shape the microbial ecosystem and affect host homoeostasis, health, and disease susceptibility. In this first Series paper, we summarise current knowledge on the microbial succession and evolutionary trajectory of the gut microbiome from neonates to adults aged 100 years and older, subsequently focusing on diurnal rhythms and seasonal patterns. We then discuss how these temporal variations in the gut microbiome are determined and how they contribute to beneficial or detrimental health outcomes in the host. Overall, elucidating the multiscale temporal dynamics of the human gut microbiome will open crucial opportunities to expand knowledge of host-microbiome interactions and their biological and clinical implications.

title: The Ethical and Legal Complexities of Regulating Companion AI Chatbots
abstract: Companion AI chatbots are increasingly used to provide friendship, emotional support, and quasi-romantic relationships, with reported benefits for loneliness and mental health. At the same time, recent suicides and other serious harms allegedly linked to such systems expose gaps in existing ethical and legal frameworks. This article interrogates these gaps through four lenses: anthropomorphism, emotional AI, emergent vulnerabilities, and mismatched legal taxonomies. First, we show how companion chatbots rely on anthropomorphic cues, creating a regulatory tension between enabling meaningful connection and avoiding deception, over-trust, and unhealthy dependency. Second, we argue that current debates on ‘emotional AI’ over-emphasise emotion recognition and under-theorise emulated empathy, where chatbots solicit self-disclosure and perform care in ways that can both support and undermine users’ autonomy. Third, we introduce the notion of emergent vulnerabilities that arise through ongoing interactions, rather than being fully specifiable ex ante, challenging legal regimes that presuppose stable vulnerability categories. Fourth, we show how instruments such as the EU AI Act misalign with the temporality, intentionality, and relational character of companion AI harms. Stepping back from these lenses, we argue for the development of a dedicated theory of harm for companion AI and propose ‘intimacy capitalism’ as a conceptual framework for analysing how firms monetise, shape, and potentially exploit digitally mediated intimate relations.

title: From Embedded Finance to Embedded Obligation: A Law-and-Economics Theory of Consumer Protection in Platform-Mediated Financial Services
abstract: Embedded finance is usually described as the integration of financial services into non-financial platforms. This article argues that the deeper regulatory transformation is the embedding of future legal and economic obligations into ordinary consumption, work, business, and entertainment environments. When credit, payment, wage-advance, wallet, and merchant-financing functions are delivered through plat form architecture, consumer protection cannot focus only on the formal product or licensed provider. It must also examine how obligations are framed, made salient or obscure, personalized through data, and allocated across platforms, merchants, lenders, and technical intermediaries. The article develops a law and-economics theory of Embedded Obligation Architecture, explains its irreducible object, refines its four layers, and distinguishes it from choice architecture, digital nudging, dark patterns, behavioral contract theory, and platform governance. It specifies the evidentiary limits of transferring behavioral evidence to embedded-finance contexts and proposes a Functional Embedded Obligation Test as a standard with gateway elements, evidentiary thresholds, legal vehicles, safe harbors, and proportionate remedies.

title: NUISS: A User-Centered Prototype for International Student Administrative Support
abstract: This study applies a streamlined User-Centered Design (UCD) approach, enhanced with AI elements, to address common administrative challenges faced by international students, such as information fragmentation and unclear processes. Using National University (NU) as a case study, the research followed core UCD stages to develop the prototype of the NU International Student Services (NUISS). These stages included task analysis informed by user stories, persona development, prototype design, and a heuristic evaluation. The high-fidelity prototype integrates three key modules-Visa Services, Certification, and Paymentwith an AI-powered assistant for proactive, personalized support. The structure is designed to enhance accessibility and minimize cognitive load. An evaluation that centered on "consistency and standards" uncovered major interface problems; this led to design refinements that boosted visual clarity and balance in a significant way. The final prototype shows that applying UCD principles in a focused way, combined with AI enhancements, can create efficient and user-friendly administrative experiences, and it offers a replicable model for similar educational contexts.

title: Mechanical instabilities in epithelial monolayers : viscoelasticity, line tension, and wetting-driven fingering
abstract: Epithelial fingering emerges when the free edge of a collectively migrating monolayer becomes mechanically unstable. Inhomogeneous line tension and spatial differences in adhesion energy relative to cell–cell cohesion guide cells toward mechanically favorable regions at finger tips. This edge-directed migration locally disrupts adherens junctions, promotes leader cell formation, and amplifies protrusive instabilities. Because fingering breaks edge continuity, it perturbs coordinated migration, weakens mechanical coherence, and compromises epithelial integrity during wound closure, morphogenesis, and barrier maintenance. Preventing fingering is therefore essential for preserving directional migration and tissue cohesion. Monolayer viscoelasticity critically regulates this behaviour. Time-dependent redistribution of residual stresses controls energy storage and dissipation and modulates effective edge line tension. Spatial variations in cell packing density generate isotropic or anisotropic migration patterns that shape in-plane stress gradients and trigger local wetting or de-wetting. Formation of a continuous supracellular actin cable increases line tension and stabilizes the edge, thereby suppressing fingering. Together, these mechanisms provide a theoretical framework linking spatial heterogeneity, viscoelastic stress redistribution, and edge mechanics to the emergence and control of epithelial fingering.

title: Diseño de un Centro de Salud Mental basado en criterios de Psicología Ambiental
abstract: La presente investigación tiene como objetivo abordar el diseño de un centro de salud mental con criterios de la psicología ambiental. Para la investigación se analizó la falta de consideración por las necesidades emocionales y psicológicas de los pacientes con trastornos de salud mental, lo cual ha llevado a la deshumanización de los espacios asistenciales, viendo cómo esta situación puede impactar negativamente en la experiencia de los usuarios y su proceso de recuperación. Se propone un Centro de Salud Mental Especializado para adultos de entre 15 y 64 años que presenten patologías como trastornos afectivos y trastornos de ansiedad. La metodología utilizada es analítica, descriptiva y factible, basada en casos internacionales, con recomendaciones por expertos en el tema. Finalmente, se sugiere la implementación de este tipo de edificación para la atención especializada de pacientes con trastornos de salud mental, debido a su contribución en la recuperación de estos pacientes al proporcionar un ambiente terapéutico y seguro, promoviendo la conexión a la naturaleza, su entorno y la interacción social.

title: Teaching Innovation Through Location Analytics: Advancing DEI and ESG in Graduate IS Curriculum
abstract: This paper presents a teaching innovation that introduces a practical, modular framework integrating Location Analytics (LA) with Diversity, Equity, and Inclusion (DEI) and Environmental, Social, and Governance (ESG) principles in graduate Information Systems (IS) education. Aligned with the Master of Science in Information Systems 2016 Global Competency Model (MSIS 2016 GCM), the framework emphasizes data, technology, business integration, and societal responsibility. It introduces a six-step pedagogical design that covers business understanding, data preparation, spatial analysis, and ethical evaluation, and embeds geospatial thinking and equity-centered reasoning into case-based learning. For IS educators, it offers ready-to-adopt teaching modules, assignments, and case studies that link technical skill development with social impact, aiming to prepare students to use data responsibly and lead with awareness of both business goals and social impact.
primary_category: Information Systems Education and Curriculum Development

title: From Flat Screens To Immersive Virtual Reality: How Virtual Reality Influences Subjective And Objective Performance In Digital Sports
abstract: Digital sports increasingly adopt Virtual Reality (VR), enabling players to perform in immersive VR conditions rather than via non-immersive screen-based conditions. Yet little is known about how this display modality shift affects subjective and objective performance. In a between-subject experimental design, 80 players completed the same digital sports task in a 3D vs. 2D condition. Direct comparisons show that the immersive VR condition improves subjective and objective performance. When presence and immersion are included as mediators, presence enhances both performance measures, whereas immersion influences only subjective performance. The direct effect of the display modality becomes non-significant, indicating that performance improvements stem from players’ experience of digital sports rather than from VR technology itself. Extending prior work that mainly examined subjective or isolated performance dimensions, this study considers both subjective and objective performance from a holistic perspective. Our findings suggest that such a display modality shift could enhance players’ performance.

analysis.中文摘要: 研究比较沉浸式VR与二维屏幕条件下玩家完成同一数字运动任务的主观与客观表现，发现VR条件提升表现，但提升主要由临场感和沉浸感等体验因素解释。

analysis.研究场景: 80名玩家在3D VR或2D屏幕条件下完成相同数字运动任务
analysis.自变量: 显示模态：3D沉浸式VR与2D非沉浸式屏幕；中介变量包括临场感和沉浸感
analysis.因变量: 主观表现、客观表现
analysis.行为指标: 数字运动任务的客观表现，具体指标未明确说明
analysis.生理/感知指标: 临场感、沉浸感、主观表现；生理指标未明确说明
analysis.研究方法: 组间实验设计；3D与2D条件对比；中介效应分析
analysis.主要结论: 沉浸式VR条件提升主观和客观表现；临场感提升两类表现，沉浸感仅影响主观表现；纳入中介变量后显示模态的直接效应不显著，说明表现提升主要来自玩家体验而非VR技术本身。

title: Empowering Governments Through The Metaverse: Building Dynamic Capabilities For Critical National Infrastructure Resilience
abstract: Critical national infrastructures face coupled disruptions—from cyberattacks and climate extremes to supply-chain cascades—that outpace current governmental coordination capabilities. This study examines the metaverse as a persistent, interoperable, and immersive layer that integrates various technologies to support resilience. Based on a structured literature review and 26 semi-structured interviews with practitioners (n=14) and metaverse experts (n=12), a framework grounded in the dynamic capabilities (sensing, seizing, and transforming) is developed. Findings suggest that metaverse-enabled systems could support governments: shared 3D operating pictures and multi-agency scenario rehearsals enhance cascade detection (sensing); scalable immersive training, virtual incident rooms, and open reference architectures enable mobilization and interoperability (seizing); and data governance, procurement, and partnership models institutionalize learning and cross-sector adoption (transforming). This study contributes to a value proposition that extends beyond isolated digital-twin pilots, offering actionable implications for policymakers, government operators, and vendors to design, govern, and scale metaverse-enabled resilience in critical national infrastructures.

analysis.中文摘要: 研究探讨元宇宙作为持久、互操作、沉浸式技术层，如何帮助政府提升关键国家基础设施面对网络攻击、气候极端和供应链级联扰动时的韧性，并构建感知、把握和转型三类动态能力。
analysis.研究主题: 元宇宙赋能政府关键基础设施韧性与动态能力建设
analysis.空间/场景类型: 元宇宙环境、共享3D操作图、多机构场景演练、虚拟事件室、关键国家基础设施场景
analysis.研究场景: 政府应对关键国家基础设施中的网络攻击、气候极端、供应链级联等复合扰动
analysis.自变量: 元宇宙使能系统、共享3D操作图、沉浸式培训、虚拟事件室、开放参考架构、数据治理与伙伴关系模式
analysis.因变量: 基础设施韧性、级联扰动检测、政府协调能力、动员能力、互操作性、制度化学习、跨部门采用
analysis.行为指标: 多机构场景演练、协同响应、事件室协作、培训参与、跨部门动员；具体量化指标未明确说明
analysis.研究方法: 结构化文献综述、26名受访者半结构化访谈、基于动态能力理论构建框架
analysis.数据/样本: 26名受访者，包括14名实践者和12名元宇宙专家；文献样本数量未明确说明
analysis.主要结论: 元宇宙系统可通过共享3D操作图和多机构演练增强级联检测，通过沉浸式培训、虚拟事件室和开放架构提升动员与互操作，通过数据治理、采购和伙伴关系模式促进组织学习和跨部门采用。
analysis.与建筑/体育空间/疗愈环境研究相关性: 具有可迁移价值。论文不直接研究建筑、体育或疗愈空间，也未涉及健康恢复或空间感知结果，但其元宇宙、3D场景、沉浸式培训、虚拟事件室和多主体协同演练方法，可借鉴到VR空间模拟、应急场景设计和复杂空间运营研究中。
analysis.可借鉴启发: 可借鉴其共享3D操作图、沉浸式场景演练、虚拟事件室和多机构协同框架，用于研究复杂空间中的应急协作、虚拟环境训练和空间运营韧性；但对行为、感知和健康结果的具体测量未明确说明。

title: Teaching German as A Foreign Language After English in A Russian-Speaking Context
abstract: The article considers the features of teaching German as a foreign language after learning English. It is shown that students who have already mastered English as a first foreign language require less effort to learn German as a second foreign language. The difficulties in learning German as a second foreign language are revealed, and the advantages are noted that should serve as an incentive for students to learn German
